mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.7% in Feb 2010 indicates post-crisis labor market recovery phase
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) directly reflects consumer spending strength and signals robust aggregate demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and equity valuations within a 5-trading-day window as market participants rapidly price in demand signals.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 355.5B in Feb 2010 shows post-recession demand recovery
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 5d returns in fewer than 58% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate near zero combined with elevated inflation expectations (CPIAUCSL) creates a negative real rate environment that incentivizes equity allocation over cash, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as portfolio rebalancing occurs.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.13% and CPIAUCSL at 217.6 in early 2010
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with real rate compression
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive 60d returns in fewer than 60% of cases, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which reduces cost pressures and supports profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.99 in Feb 2010 shows post-recession industrial recovery
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20d returns in fewer than 56% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal broad-based demand recovery across residential and consumption sectors. When both accelerate simultaneously, the multiplier effect on corporate earnings expectations drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 591K and RSAFS at 355.5B in early 2010
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20d returns in fewer than 62% of cases, or the joint interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates or turns negative, signaling labor market weakness that reduces consumer income and spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 129.5M in Feb 2010 shows ongoing post-crisis employment weakness
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20d returns in fewer than 52% of cases, or Rank IC > -0.02 (fails to show negative predictive power).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains at the zero lower bound (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2% year-over-year, real rates become deeply negative, creating a regime where equity risk premiums compress and S&P 500 valuations expand, producing positive returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Deep negative real rates in 2010 post-crisis period
regime_hint: zero-rate monetary accommodation with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive 60d returns in fewer than 58% of cases, or the real-rate proxy (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL inflation) produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly signals the underlying strength of aggregate demand and economic expansion. Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13161.1B in Q3 2009 shows early recovery from Great Recession
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60d returns in fewer than 62% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
